ref: and named it “Sky.” Evening came, then morning—that was the second day. The Third Day
text: Na Nyankopɔn frɛɛ wimu no ɔsor. Na adze sae na adze kyee, da a otsia ebien nye no.
translated: And God called the firmament Heaven. And the evening and the morning were the second day.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Na Nyankopɔn bɔɔ nyimpa wɔ nankasa no su do, Nyew, Nyankopɔn no su do na ɔbɔɔ nyimpa; ɔbɔɔ hɔn ɔbanyin nye ɔbaa.
translated: And God created mankind in his own image, Yes, in the image of God he created mankind; he created them male and female.

ref: I have provided all kinds of fruit and grain for you to eat.
text: Na Nyankopɔn see dɛ, Hwɛ, mama hom nduadzewa na nduaba a ɔwɔ asaase nyinara enyi, dɛ ɔnyɛ edziban mma hom:
translated: And God said, Behold, I have given you every herb bearing seed, which is upon the face of all the earth, and every tree, in the which is the fruit of a tree yielding seed; to you it shall be for meat:

ref: So the heavens and the earth and everything else were created. The Seventh Day
text: Na wowiee sor nye asaase nye dza ɔwɔ mu nyinara yɛ.
translated: And he created the heavens and the earth and all that is in them.

ref: God blessed the seventh day and made it special, because on that day he rested from his work.
text: Na Nyankopɔn hyiraa da a otsia esuon no do na ɔtseew ho: osiandɛ ɔno mu na ɔhomee fii no ndwuma a ɔayɛ nyinara ho. Eden Ture
translated: And God blessed the seventh day and made it holy: because on it he rested from all the work he had done. Genesis

ref: The Lord God put the man in the Garden of Eden to take care of it and to look after it.
text: Na EWURADZE Nyankopɔn faa nyimpa no, na ɔdze no kɔtoo Eden ture no mu dɛ onsiesie hɔ, na ɔnhwɛ hɔ.
translated: And the LORD God took the man, and put him into the garden of Eden to dress it and to keep it.

ref: except the one that has the power to let you know the difference between right and wrong. If you eat any fruit from that tree, you will die before the day is over!”
text: na papa nye bɔn ho nyimdzee dua no dze, mma nndzi bi: na da ara a ibedzi bi no, ampaara dɛ ibowu.
translated: and father is not ashamed of the town, let him not say anything: and the day that you will say it, truly you will die.

ref: You will sweat all your life to earn a living; you were made out of soil, and you will once again turn into soil.”
text: w’enyim mfifir mu na ibedzidzi kɛpem dɛ ebɛsan akɔ daadze; osiandɛ ɔno mu na woyii wo fii: na huntuma nye wo, na huntuma mu na ebɛsan akɔ.
translated: your face will be darkened and you will be ashamed and go back home; because from there you were born: and dust you are, and to dust you shall return.

ref: and Abel also gave an offering to the Lord. He killed the first-born lamb from one of his sheep and gave the Lord the best parts of it. The Lord was pleased with Abel and his offering,
text: na Abel so dze no nguan mu mbakan na dza wɔadodɔ seradze no mu bi bae. Na EWURADZE n’enyi sɔɔ Abel nye n’afɔrbɔ:
translated: and Abel also brought some of the firstborn of his flock and of their fat portions. And the LORD looked with favor upon Abel and his offering:

ref: but not with Cain and his offering. This made Cain so angry that he could not hide his feelings.
text: na Kain nye n’afɔrbɔ dze n’enyi annsɔ. Na Kain no bo fuuw kɛsenara, na omunae.
translated: but Cain and his offering did not please him. So Cain was very angry, and his face fell.

ref: “This punishment is too hard!” Cain said.
text: Na Kain see EWURADZE dɛ, Mo bɔn ho akatua so kyɛn dɛ mubotum asoa.
translated: And Cain said to the LORD, My punishment is greater than I can bear.

ref: Then Enoch had a son named Irad, who had a son named Mehujael, who had a son named Methushael, who had a son named Lamech.
text: Na Enok woo Irad; na Irad woo Mehujael: na Mehujael woo Metusael: na Metusael woo Lamek.
translated: And Enoch begat Irad; and Irad begat Mehujael: and Mehujael begat Methusael: and Methusael begat Lamech.

ref: Lamech and Zillah had a son named Tubal Cain who made tools out of bronze and iron. They also had a daughter, whose name was Naamah.
text: Na Zila, so woo Tubal-kain a ɔton kɔɔber nye dadze ndzɛmba a wɔdze twa adze nyinara no; na Tubal-kain n’akyerɛbaa nye Naama.
translated: And Zillah, she also bore Tubal-cain, a forger of all instruments of bronze and iron; and Tubal-cain's sister was Naamah.

ref: Later, Seth had a son and named him Enosh. About this time people started worshiping the Lord.
text: Na Set so, wɔwoo babanyin maa no; na ɔtoo no dzin Enos: nna afei adasa hyɛɛ ase bɔɔ EWURADZE ne dzin sɔɔr no.
translated: And to Seth also, a son was born; and he named him Enos: then men began to call upon the name of the LORD.

ref: and died at the age of
text: na Kenan ne nda nyinara yɛɛ mfe ahaakron nye du: na owui.
translated: and Kenan lived nine hundred and ten years in all: and he died.

ref: and died at the age of
text: Mahalalel ne nda nyinara yɛɛ mfe ahaawɔtwe eduokron enum.
translated: Mahalalel lived a total of eight hundred and ninety-five years.

ref: He was sorry that he had made them,
text: EWURADZE nuu noho dɛ ɔyɛɛ nyimpa wɔ asaase do, na ɔyɛɛ no yaw wɔ n’akoma mu.
translated: The LORD saw that the wickedness of man was great on the earth, and it grieved him in his heart.

ref: Take seven pairs of every kind of animal that can be used for sacrifice and one pair of all others.
text: Mbowa a hɔn ho tsew nyinara fa hɔn mu esuon esuon, onyin nye ne ber; na mbowa a hɔn ho nntsew no mu ebien ebien, onyin na ne ber;
translated: Of the animals that are clean, take seven pairs of each, male and female; and of the animals that are not clean, take two pairs, male and female;

ref: Then on the seventeenth day of the seventh month of the year, the boat came to rest somewhere in the Ararat mountains.
text: Na ehɛn no kesii Ararat nkokwaa do wɔ bosoom a otsia esuon no mu, bosoom no ne da a otsia duesuon no mu.
translated: And the ark rested upon the mountains of Ararat in the seventh month, on the seventeenth day of the month.

ref: All animals, birds, reptiles, and fish will be afraid of you. I have placed them under your control,
text: Na hom ho suro nye hom ho hu mbra asaase do mbowadoma nyinara nye wimu ntumbowa nyinara do; dza wɔwea daadze nyinara, na po mu nam nyinara, wɔdze ahyɛ hom nsa.
translated: And the fear of you and the dread of you shall be upon every beast of the earth and upon every bird of the heavens; upon everything that creeps on the ground, and all the fish of the sea, they are delivered into your hand.

ref: Again, God said to Noah and his sons:
text: Na Nyankopɔn kasaa kyerɛɛ Noa, nye ne mba a wɔka no ho no dɛ,
translated: And God spoke to Noah, and his sons who were with him, saying,

ref: When I send clouds over the earth, and a rainbow appears in the sky,
text: Na ɔbɛba dɛ, sɛ medze mununkum meba asaase do a, wobohu nyankontɔn no wɔ mununkum no mu,
translated: And it shall come to pass, when I bring a cloud over the earth, that the bow shall be seen in the cloud,

ref: Serug had more children and died at the age of
text: na Nahor n’ekyir no odzii mfe ahaenu, na ɔwoo mbabanyin nye mbabaa.
translated: And after Nahor was born, he lived 119 years and had sons and daughters.

ref: but the Lord appeared to Abram and promised, “I will give this land to your family forever.” Abram then built an altar there for the Lord.
text: Na EWURADZE yii noho kyerɛɛ Abram, na ɔsee dɛ, W’asefo na medze asaase yi bɛma hɔn: na hɔ na Abram sii afɔrmbukyia maa EWURADZE a oyii noho kyerɛɛ no no.
translated: And the LORD appeared unto Abram, and said, Unto thy seed will I give this land: and there builded Abram an altar unto the LORD, who appeared unto him.

ref: So the king told his men to let Abram and Sarai take their possessions and leave.
text: Na Farao hyɛɛ nkorɔfo ma wokegyaa ɔno, nye ne yer, nye dza ɔwɔ nyinara wɔ ɔkwan do.
translated: And Pharaoh commanded his men concerning him, and they sent him away with his wife and all that he had on the way.

ref: Abram said to Lot, “We are close relatives. We shouldn't argue, and our men shouldn't be fighting one another.
text: Na Abram see Lot dɛ, Meserɛ wo, mma emma aperper mmba emi nye ɔwo ntamu, na mo mbowahwɛfo nye wo mbowahwɛfo ntamu; osiandɛ yɛyɛ enuanom.
translated: And Abram said to Lot, Please let there be no strife between me and you, and between my herdsmen and your herdsmen; for we are brethren.

ref: So Lot chose the whole Jordan Valley for himself, and as he started out toward the east, he and Abram separated.
text: Nna Lot faa Jordan Tɛtɛrmu nyinara; na Lot tui faa boka afamu: na ɔbaako paa noho fii ne nyɛnko ho.
translated: Then Lot chose all the plain of Jordan; and Lot journeyed east: and they separated themselves the one from the other.

ref: where the people were evil and sinned terribly against the Lord. Abram Moves to Hebron
text: Afei Sodomfo yɛ ehuhufo nye ndzebɔnyɛfo kɛsenara tsia EWURADZE.
translated: Now the people of Sodom were wicked and great sinners against the LORD.

ref: I will give you more descendants than there are specks of dust on the earth, and someday it will be easier to count those specks of dust than to count your descendants.
text: Na mebɛma w’asefo ayɛ dɛ asaase do huntuma: na sɛ obi tum kan asaase do huntuma a, ɔnodze nna wobotum akan w’asefo no.
translated: And I will give to your descendants all the land of the earth: and if anyone tramples on the land of the earth, he will not be able to trample on your descendants.

ref: A year later King Chedorlaomer and his allies attacked and defeated the Rephaites in Ashteroth-Karnaim, the Zuzites in Ham, and the Emites in Shaveh-Kiriathaim.
text: Na afe a otsia duanan no mu na Kedorlaomer na ahemfo a wɔka no ho no bae, na wobokum Refafo wɔ Asterot-karnaim, nye Zuzfo wɔ Ham, nye Emfo wɔ Savɛ-kiriataim,
translated: And in the fourteenth year Chedorlaomer and the kings that were with him came and defeated the Rephaites in Ashteroth-karnaim, and the Zuzites in Ham, and the Emites in Shaveh-kiriathaim,

ref: They also captured Abram's nephew Lot, who lived in Sodom. They took him and his possessions and then left.
text: Na wɔfaa Lot, Abram no nua ba a ɔtse Sodom no, nye ne ndzɛmba, na wosii mu kɔree.
translated: And they took Lot, Abram's nephew who lived in Sodom, and his possessions, and went away.

ref: Let my share be the food that my men have eaten. But Aner, Eshcol, and Mamre went with me, so give them their share of what we brought back.
text: gyedɛ dza mberantsɛ no edzi no nko, na Aner, Eskol nye Mamre a wɔnye me kɔree no hɔn kyɛpɛn.
translated: Let the young men alone eat what they have taken, except the portion of the men who went with me—Aner, Eshcol and Mamre—let them take their share.

ref: and Sarai gave him Hagar to be his wife. This happened after Abram had lived in the land of Canaan for ten years.
text: Na Abram tsenaa Keenan asaase do mfe du n’ekyir no, Abram ne yer Sarai faa n’afonabaa Hagar, Egyptnyi no, na ɔdze no maa no kun Abram awar.
translated: And when Abram had lived in the land of Canaan for ten years, Abram's wife Sarai took Hagar the Egyptian, her maidservant, and gave her to Abram her husband as a wife.

ref: I will keep my solemn promise to you and give you more descendants than can be counted.”
text: Na mebɛma m’ahyɛmu ada emi nye ɔwo ntamu, na mebɛma adɔr mbordo.
translated: And I will give my blessing to you and your descendants, and I will make you a great nation.

ref: I will give you a lot of descendants, and they will become great nations. Some of them will even be kings.
text: Na mebɛma awo mbordo, na mayɛ wo amanaman, na ahemfo efi wo mu epue.
translated: I will make you very fruitful, and I will make you into nations, and kings shall come from you.

ref: God finished speaking to Abraham and then left.
text: Na ɔnye no gyaa kasa no, Nyankopɔn fii Abraham nkyɛn kɔr sor.
translated: And when he finished speaking, God left Abraham and went up to heaven.

ref: and said, “Please come to my home where I can serve you.
text: na ɔsee dɛ, Mo wura, sɛ afei menya w’enyim enyimnyam a, meserɛ wo, mma mmpa w’akowaa ho nnkɔ.
translated: and he said, My Lord, if now I have found favor in your sight, I pray you, do not pass by your servant.

ref: The sun was coming up as Lot reached the town of Zoar,
text: Na aber a Lot dur Zoar no, nna ewia epue asaase do.
translated: And when Lot entered Zoar, the sun had risen upon the earth.

ref: That same morning Abraham got up and went to the place where he had stood and spoken with the Lord.
text: Na Abraham soɛɛr anapaatu kɔr mbrɛ ogyinae wɔ EWURADZE n’enyim no:
translated: And Abraham stood yet before the LORD:

ref: One day his older daughter said to her sister, “Our father is old, and there are no men anywhere for us to marry.
text: Na abakan no see ekumaba no dɛ, Hɛn egya ayɛ akɔdaa, na ɔbanyin biara nnyi asaase do a ɔbɛba hɛn nkyɛn dɛ mbrɛ asaase nyinara yɛ no:
translated: And the youngest said to the father, Father, give me the portion of goods that falls to me. And he divided unto them his living.

ref: Then God continued: I know you are innocent. That's why I kept you from sleeping with Sarah and doing anything wrong.
text: Na Nyankopɔn see no adaaso mu, dɛ, ɛhɛɛ, minyim dɛ w’akoma mu tsewee mu na ayɛ yi, na mo so musuoo wo mu amma aannyɛ bɔn enntsia me: dɛm ntsi na mamma annka no.
translated: And God saw it in his heart, that, yes, I know that your heart's intention in this is good, and you did not think evil in yourselves to sin against me: that is why I did not allow it to happen.

ref: What were you thinking when you did this?
text: Na Abimelek bisaa Abraham dɛ, Ebɛnadze na ihunii, ma ayɛ dɛm adze yi?
translated: And Abimelech asked Abraham, "What was your reason for doing this thing?"

ref: When God made us leave my father's home and start wandering, I told her, “If you really love me, then tell everyone that I am your brother.”
text: Na ɔbaa dɛ aber a Nyankopɔn maa mifii m’egya fie mikekyinii no, nna mesee ɔbaa no dɛ, Ayamuyie a fa yɛ me nye yi; beebiara mbrɛ yebodu no, ka no wɔ mo ho dɛ, Mo nua a.
translated: And when the woman whom God gave me from my father's house, the one I married, when I married that woman, this is the kindness I ask of you; wherever we arrive, say of me that, He is my brother.

ref: Meanwhile, God had kept Abimelech's wife and slaves from having children. But Abraham prayed, and God let them start having children again.
text: Osiandɛ nna EWURADZE esiw Abimelek ne fie yafun nyinara mu kwan petsee wɔ Abraham ne yer Sara ntsi.
translated: For the LORD had closed up all the wombs of the house of Abimelech because of Sarah, Abraham's wife.

ref: The time came when Sarah no longer had to nurse Isaac, and on that day Abraham gave a big feast. Hagar and Ishmael Are Sent Away
text: Na abofra no nyinii, na wotwaa no enumfo: na da a wotwaa Isaak enumfo no, Abraham too pon kɛsenara.
translated: And the child grew, and was weaned: and the day that Isaac was weaned, Abraham made a great feast.

ref: Help him up and hold his hand, because I will make him the father of a great nation.”
text: Soɛr, kɛma abofra no do, na fa wo nsa suo no mu; na mebɛyɛ no ɔman kɛse.
translated: Arise, lift up the lad, and hold him in thine hand; for I will make him a great nation.

ref: God blessed Ishmael, and as the boy grew older, he became an expert at hunting with his bow and arrows. He lived in the Paran Desert, and his mother chose an Egyptian woman for him to marry. A Peace Treaty
text: Na ɔtsenaa Paran sar do; na ne na kɔwaar yer wɔ Egypt asaase do brɛɛ no. Abraham Nye Abimelek Dzi Ahyɛmu
translated: And he dwelt in the wilderness of Paran; and his mother took him a wife out of the land of Egypt. Abraham and Abimelech Make a Covenant

ref: “But sir,” the man replied, “the property is worth only
text: Mo wura, tsie me: dwetɛ dwetɛbona ahaanan asaase nye dɛn wɔ emi na ɔwo ntamu? Dɛm ntsi sie wo fun.
translated: My lord, listen to me: the land is worth four hundred shekels of silver, but what is that between me and you? Therefore bury your dead.

ref: One day, Abraham called in his most trusted servant and said to him, “Solemnly promise me
text: Na Abraham see, ne fie somfo panyin a ɔhwɛ adze a ɔwɔ nyinara do no, dɛ, Meserɛ wo, fa wo nsa to m’aserɛ ase:
translated: And Abraham said to his eldest servant of his house, who ruled over all that he had, Please, I beg you, put your hand under my thigh:

ref: After that, they brought in food. But the servant said, “Before I eat, I must tell you why I have come.” “Go ahead and tell us,” Laban answered.
text: Na wɔdze edziban besii n’enyim dɛ ondzi: na ɔsee dɛ, Minnkedzi, gyedɛ mabɔ m’amandzɛɛ ana. Na ɔsee dɛ, Bɔ ɛ.
translated: And they brought a denarius to him and he said: Whose image and inscription is this? And he said, Tell it.

ref: Instead, go back to the land where I was born and find a wife for my son from among my relatives.”
text: Kɔ m’egya fie, nye m’ebusua mu, na kɛgye yer ma me ba.
translated: Go to my father's house, to my family, and find a wife for my son.

ref: If she gives me a drink and offers to get some water for my camels, I'll know she is the one you have chosen.”
text: na ɔbɛse me dɛ, ɔwoara nom bi, na mebɛsaw bi ama w’efumpɔnkɔ so no: ɔnoara na ma ɔnyɛ ɔbaa a EWURADZE eyi no ama mo wura ne ba.
translated: and she will give me to drink, and she will also draw water for your camels: let her be the woman whom the LORD has appointed for my master's son.

ref: because the Lord had appeared to Isaac and said: Isaac, stay away from Egypt! I will show you where I want you to go.
text: Na EWURADZE yii noho kyerɛɛ no, na ɔsee dɛ, Mma nnkɔ Egypt; tsena asaase a mebɛkyerɛ wo no do.
translated: And the LORD appeared unto him, and said, Go not down into Egypt; dwell in the land which I shall tell thee of.

ref: Isaac replied, “I am old and might die at any time.
text: Na ɔsee dɛ, Afei hwɛ, mayɛ akɔdaa, minnyim mo wu da.
translated: And he said, Now look, I have become a child, I don't know the day you will die.

ref: “My brother Esau is a hairy man,” Jacob reminded her. “And I am not.
text: Na Jakob see ne na Rebeka dɛ, Hwɛ, mo nua Esau no ho wɔ nhwi, na emi mo ho yɛ monomonom.
translated: And Jacob said to his mother Rebekah, Behold, my brother Esau is a hairy man, and I am a smooth man.

ref: Jacob replied, “I am Esau, your first-born, and I have done what you told me. Please sit up and eat the meat I have brought. Then you can give me your blessing.”
text: Na Jakob see n’egya dɛ, Emi nye w’abakan Esau; mayɛ dɛ mbrɛ ekyerɛɛ me no: soɛr meserɛ wo, tsena hɔ na dzi me hanam yi bi, ama wo kra eehyira me.
translated: And Jacob said to his father, I am Esau your firstborn; I have done as you told me: please arise, sit up and eat some of my meat, so that your soul may bless me.

ref: Nations will be your servants and bow down to you. You will rule over your brothers, and they will kneel at your feet. Anyone who curses you will be cursed; anyone who blesses you will be blessed.”
text: Aman nsom wo, Na amanaman nkotow wo: Yɛ wo nuanom do wura, Na wo na mba nkotow wo: Ndom nka hɔn a wɔdom wo nyinara, Na nhyira nka hɔn a wohyira wo nyinara. Esau Bisa Nhyira
translated: Let nations serve you, And peoples bow down to you: Be lord over your brothers, And let your mother's sons bow down to you: Cursed be those who curse you, And blessed be those who bless you. Esau Asks for a Blessing

ref: Isaac called in Jacob, then gave him a blessing, and said: Don't marry any of those Canaanite women.
text: Na Isaak frɛɛ Jakob, na ohyiraa no, na ɔhyɛɛ no, na ɔsee no dɛ, Mma nnwar yer wɔ Keenan mbabaa mu.
translated: And Isaac called Jacob, and blessed him, and charged him, and said unto him, Thou shalt not take a wife of the daughters of Canaan.

ref: The Lord was standing beside the ladder and said: I am the Lord God who was worshiped by Abraham and Isaac. I will give to you and your family the land on which you are now sleeping.
text: Na, hwɛ, EWURADZE gyina n’etsifi, na ɔsee dɛ, Emi nye EWURADZE, w’egya Abraham ne Nyankopɔn, na Isaak ne Nyankopɔn: asaase a eda do no, medze mebɛma ɔwo, nye w’asefo.
translated: And, behold, the LORD stood above it, and said, I am the LORD God of thy father Abraham, and the God of Isaac: the land whereon thou liest, to thee will I give it, and to thy seed.

ref: Your descendants will spread over the earth in all directions and will become as numerous as the specks of dust. Your family will be a blessing to all people.
text: Na w’asefo bɛyɛ dɛ asaase do huntuma, na ebɛtserɛw akɔ anee, boka, nye kwaamu, nye pomu: na ɔwo nye w’asefo mu na wobehyira asaase do ebusua ebusua nyinara.
translated: And your descendants will be like the dust of the earth, and you will spread abroad to the west, and to the east, and to the north, and to the south: and in you and in your descendants shall all the families of the earth be blessed.

ref: Jacob asked the shepherds, “Where are you from?” “We're from Haran,” they answered.
text: Na Jakob bisaa hɔn dɛ, Mo nuanom, hom fi hen? Na wobuaa dɛ, Yefi Haran.
translated: And Jacob asked them, "My brothers, where are you from?" And they answered, "We are from Haran."

ref: “How is he?” Jacob asked. “He's fine,” they answered. “And here comes his daughter Rachel with the sheep.”
text: Na obisaa hɔn dɛ, No ho ye? Na wɔsee dɛ, No ho ye: na, hwɛ, ne babaa Rahel na ɔnye nguan reba yi.
translated: And he asked them, "Is he well?" And they said, "He is well; and behold, Rachel his daughter is coming with the sheep."

ref: But that evening he brought Leah to Jacob, who married her and spent the night with her.
text: Na ɔbaa dɛ anafua no ɔfaa ne babaa Leah, na ɔdze no brɛɛ no; na ɔnye no dae
translated: And it came to pass in the morning that it was Leah, and he said to him; what is this you have done to me

ref: She had one more son and named him Judah, because she said, “I'll praise the Lord!”
text: Na onyinseen bio, na ɔwoo babanyin: na ɔsee dɛ, Afei mibeyi EWURADZE ayɛw: dɛm ntsi ɔfrɛɛ ne dzin Juda; a asekyerɛ nye; Ayɛyi na ogyaa awoo.
translated: And she conceived again, and bore a son: and she said, Now will I praise the LORD: therefore she called his name Judah; which means; Praise and she left bearing.

ref: When Bilhah and Jacob had a second son,
text: Na Rahel n’abaawa Bilha nyinseen bio, na ɔwoo babanyin a otsia ebien maa Jakob.
translated: And Rachel's maid Bilhah conceived again, and bore Jacob a second son.

ref: “I'm really lucky,” Leah said, and she named the boy Gad.
text: Na Leah see dɛ, Siar aba, na ɔfrɛɛ ne dzin Gad a asekyerɛ nye, Yiedzi.
translated: And when Leah saw that she had stopped bearing children, she took her maid Zilpah and gave her to Jacob as a wife.

ref: After Joseph was born, Jacob said to Laban, “Release me from our agreement and let me return to my own country.
text: Na ɔbaa dɛ aber a Rahel woo Josef no, nna Jakob see Laban dɛ, Gya me kwan ma monkɔ mbrɛ mifi na me man mu.
translated: And it came to pass when Rachel had given birth to Joseph, that Jacob said to Laban, Send me away that I may go to my place and my country.

ref: Some of the sheep that Jacob was keeping for Laban were already spotted. And when the others were ready to mate, he made sure that they faced in the direction of the spotted and black ones. In this way, Jacob built up a flock of sheep for himself and did not put them with the other sheep.
text: Na Jakob paa nguambaa no na ɔmaa nguan no hɔn enyim kɔr Laban nguan no mu nsensanee nsensanee nye etuntum no nyinara hɔn do; na ɔyɛɛ narankasa no nguankuw wɔ nkyɛn, na ɔammfa ammfora Laban no nguan no mu.
translated: And Jacob separated the lambs and made the sheep face toward the striped and all the dark ones in Laban's flock; and he made separate flocks for himself, and did not put them with Laban's sheep.

ref: years to buy your sheep and goats. During that time you kept changing my wages.
text: Na homara nyim dɛ m’ahoɔdzen nyinara na medze asom hom egya.
translated: And all my strength and all my abilities that I use to serve you all.

ref: and I am the God you worshiped at Bethel, when you poured olive oil on a rock and made a promise to me. Leave here at once and return to the land where you were born.”
text: Emi nye Bɛtɛl Nyankopɔn, beebi mbrɛ eseraa idum ho no a, beebi mbrɛ ebɔɔ me anohoba no a; afei soɛr, fi asaase yi do, na san kɔ w’awobea asaase do.
translated: I am the God of Bethel, where you anointed a pillar and where you made a vow to me. Now leave this land at once and go back to your native land.

ref: Jacob became very angry and said to Laban: What have I done wrong? Have I committed some crime? Is that why you hunted me down?
text: Na Jakob no bo fuwii, na ɔnye Laban hamee: na Jakob buae see Laban dɛ, Mo mfom nye mmuana? Mo bɔn nye mmuana, ma etsiw me ɔhyew do dɛm yi?
translated: And Jacob became angry, and he quarreled with Laban: and Jacob answered and said to Laban, What is my trespass? What is my sin, that you have so hotly pursued after me?

ref: If the fearsome God worshiped by Abraham and my father Isaac had not been on my side, you would have sent me away without a thing. But God saw my hard work, and he knew the trouble I was in, so he helped me. Then last night he told you how wrong you were. Jacob and Laban Make an Agreement
text: Sɛ m’egya ne Nyankopɔn, Abraham ne Nyankopɔn, nye Isaak no Suro, enndzi m’ekyir a, nkyɛ ndɛ egya me kwan nsapan. Nyankopɔn ehu m’amandzehu nye me nsaano edwumayɛ, na anadwe yi ɔaka w’enyim. Jakob Na Laban Dzi Ahyɛmu
translated: If the God of my father, the God of Abraham, and the Fear of Isaac, had not been with me, you would surely have sent me away empty-handed. But God has seen my hardship and the labor of my hands, and last night he rebuked you. Jacob and Laban Make a Covenant

ref: After Jacob had set up a large rock,
text: Na Jakob faa ɔbo, na ɔdze yɛɛ idum sii hɔ.
translated: And Jacob took a stone, and set it up as a pillar.

ref: Jacob sent messengers on ahead to Esau, who lived in the land of Seir, also known as Edom.
text: Na Jakob tuu abɔfo ma wodzii n’enyim kan kɔr no nua Esau hɔ wɔ Seir asaase do, Edom haban mu.
translated: And Jacob sent messengers before him to his brother Esau in the land of Seir, in the country of Edom.

ref: So tell him, ‘They belong to your servant Jacob, who is coming this way. He is sending them as a gift to his master Esau.’ ”
text: Ɔno se dɛ, W’akowaa Jakob dze a; ɔyɛ akyɛdze a wɔdze rekɛma mo wura Esau: na, hwɛ, ɔnoara so dzi hɛn ekyir.
translated: He said, "These belong to your servant Jacob; they are a gift being sent to my lord Esau: and behold, he himself is also behind us."

ref: The man said, “From now on, your name will no longer be Jacob. You will be called Israel, because you have wrestled with God and with men, and you have won.”
text: Na ɔsee dɛ, Wɔnnkɛfrɛ wo dzin Jakob bio, na mbom, Israel: osiandɛ ɔwo enye Nyankopɔn na ɔnye nyimpa ako, na edzi konyim.
translated: And he said, Your name shall no longer be called Jacob, but Israel: for you have struggled with God and with men, and have prevailed.

ref: As Jacob was on his way back home, some of God's angels came and met him.
text: Na Jakob sii no kwan do, na Nyankopɔn n’abɔfoe behyiaa no.
translated: And when Jacob went on his way, the angels of God met him.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Na Jakob hun hɔn no ɔsee dɛ, Nyankopɔn no dɔm nye yi: na ɔfrɛɛ bea no ne dzin Mahanaim.
translated: And when Jacob saw them, he said, "This is God's camp," and he called the name of that place Mahanaim.

ref: Afterwards, Jacob went back and spent the rest of the night alone. A man came and fought with Jacob until just before daybreak.
text: Na ɔkaa Jakob no nko; na banyin bi nye no dzii aper wɔ hɔ ara ma okodur adzekyee.
translated: And he left Jacob alone; and a certain man wrestled with him there until daybreak.

ref: The man said, “From now on, your name will no longer be Jacob. You will be called Israel, because you have wrestled with God and with men, and you have won.”
text: Na ɔsee dɛ, Wɔnnkɛfrɛ wo dzin Jakob bio, na mbom, Israel: osiandɛ ɔwo enye Nyankopɔn na ɔnye nyimpa ako, na edzi konyim.
translated: And he said, Your name shall no longer be called Jacob, but Israel: for you have struggled with God and with men, and have prevailed.

ref: Jacob was so frightened that he divided his people, sheep, cattle, and camels into two groups.
text: Nna Jakob n’akoma tuu kɛsenara, na no ho hiahiaa no. Na obuee nkorɔfo a wɔnye no nam no, nye mpɔnkye, nguan, anantwi nye efumpɔnkɔ no hɔn mu ekuw ebien;
translated: So Jacob became very rich, and he had many servants, and sheep, goats, cattle and donkeys were divided into two groups;

ref: Esau replied, “Let me leave some of my men with you.” “You don't have to do that,” Jacob answered. “I am happy, simply knowing that you are friendly to me.”
text: Na Esau see dɛ, Nna ma memfa nyimpa a wɔka mo ho yi bi mingya wo. Na ɔsee dɛ, Ma ɔnyɛ dɛn? Wɔmma minnya mo wura n’enyim enyimnyam.
translated: And Esau said, Father, let me not take any of these people who are with you. And he said, What shall I do? Let me find favor in my lord's sight.

ref: But Jacob went to Succoth, where he built a house for himself and set up shelters for his animals. That's why the place is called Succoth. Jacob Arrives at Shechem
text: Na Jakob sii mu kɔr Sukɔt, na okesii dan maa noho, na osisii mpata maa no mbowa: dɛm ntsi wɔfrɛɛ bea no ne dzin Sukɔt.
translated: And Jacob went to Succoth, and he built a house for himself, and made booths for his cattle: therefore the name of the place is called Succoth.

ref: The land where he camped was owned by the descendants of Hamor, the father of Shechem. So Jacob paid them
text: Na ɔtɔɔ asaase kakraba a ɔbɔɔ n’asesee wɔ do no, wɔ Sekem n’egya Hamor ne mba no hɔ, maa dwetɛ ɔha.
translated: And he bought the piece of land where he had pitched his tent from the sons of Hamor, Shechem's father, for a hundred pieces of silver.

ref: These people really are friendly. Why not let them move freely about until they find the property they want? There's enough land here for them and for us. Then our families can marry into theirs, and theirs can marry into ours.
text: Mbarimba yi wɔnye hɛn tse no asomdwee mu; dɛm ntsi hom mma wɔntsena asaase yi do, na wondzi mu gua; na, hwɛ asaase yi dɔɔ so ara yie ma hɔn; hom mma yɛmfa hɔn mbabaa nwar hɔn, na wɔmma hɛn so yɛmfa hɛn mbabaa mma hɔn.
translated: Let us dwell with them in the land in peace; therefore let them dwell in the land, and trade therein; for, behold, the land is large enough for them; let us take their daughters to us for wives, and let us give them our daughters.

ref: They took sheep, goats, donkeys, and everything else that was in the town or the countryside.
text: Wɔfaa hɔn nguan nye hɔn anantwi, nye hɔn nsoasopɔnkɔ nye dza ɔwɔ kurow no mu, na dza ɔwɔ haban mu;
translated: They took their sheep and their cattle, and their donkeys and what was in the city, and what was in the field;

ref: Rachel was at the point of death, and right before dying, she said, “I'll name him Benoni.” But Jacob called him Benjamin.
text: Na ɔbaa dɛ, ne kra rifi no mu no , ɔtoo no dzin Ben-oni; na n’egya dze ɔfrɛɛ no Benjamin.
translated: And when the woman's soul was departing from her, she named him Ben-oni; but his father called him Benjamin.

ref: Esau and his three wives had five sons while in Canaan. Adah's son was Eliphaz; Basemath's son was Reuel; Oholibamah's three sons were Jeush, Jalam, and Korah.
text: na Oholibama woo Jeus, Jalam, nye Kora: woyinom nye Esau ne mba a wɔwoo hɔn wɔ Keenan asaase do no.
translated: and Oholibamah bore Jeush, Jalam, and Korah: these are the sons of Esau who were born to him in the land of Canaan.

ref: All of these clans descended from Esau, who was known as Edom.

translated: These are the descendants of Esau, who is also called Edom, and these are their chiefs. The descendants of Seir (

ref: Dishon's sons were Hemdan, Eshban, Ithran, and Cheran.
text: Na woyinom nye Dison ne mba; Hemdan, Esban, Itran nye Keran.
translated: And these are the sons of Dishon; Hemdan, Eshban, Ithran and Cheran.

ref: The clans of the Horites were Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah,
text: Ekunyin a wofi Horfo mu puei nye woyinom; ekunyin Lotan, Sobal, Zibeon, na Ana,
translated: The sons of Seir the Horite were these: Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, and Anah,

ref: Joseph's brothers were jealous of him, but his father kept wondering about the dream. Joseph Is Sold and Taken to Egypt
text: Na no nuanom enyi beer no do; na n’egya dze ɔdze asɛm no too ne tsirmu. Israel Soma Josef Kɔ No Nuanom Nkyɛn
translated: And his brothers were jealous of him; but his father kept the matter in mind. Israel Sent Joseph to His Brothers

ref: They said to one another, “Look, here comes the hero of those dreams!
text: Na wɔsesee hɔnho dɛ, Wɔnhwɛ, ɔdaasofo no na ɔreba no.
translated: And when they saw him from afar, Behold, the dreamer is coming.

ref: Judah asked his friend Hirah take a goat to the woman, so he could get back the ring and walking stick, but she wasn't there.
text: Na Juda dze apɔnkyeba no maa ne nyɛnko Adullamnyi no, dɛ ɔmfa nkɛgye ahobadze no wɔ ɔbaa no nsa mu: naaso oennhu no.
translated: And Judah sent the young goat by his friend the Adullamite, to receive the pledge from the woman's hand: but he did not find her.

ref: Tamar later gave birth to twins. But before either of them was born, one of them stuck a hand out of her womb. The woman who was helping tied a red thread around the baby's hand and explained, “This one came out first.”
text: Na ɔbaa dɛ aber a ɔrowo no, nna kor dze ne nsa puei; na awogyefo no faa ahoma memen dze kyekyeer ne nsa dɛ, Oyi na odzii kan puei.
translated: And when the woman was in labor giving birth, one put out his hand; and the midwife took a scarlet thread and tied it on his hand saying, This one came out first.

ref: She kept begging Joseph day after day, but he refused to do what she wanted or even to go near her.
text: Na ɔbaa dɛ, daa daa nyinara ɔkaa kyerɛɛ Josef, naaso ɔanngye no pen, dɛ ɔnye no bɛda, anaa ɔnye no bɛyɛ biribi.
translated: And it happened that day after day she spoke to Joseph, but he did not listen to her to lie with her or to be with her.

ref: One night each of the two men had a dream, but their dreams had different meanings.
text: Na hɔn beenu nyina sosoo daa, anafua da kor a ɔsor obiara ne daa, nye ne nkyerɛasee.
translated: And both of them were always together, every morning and evening, day and night.

ref: I held the king's cup and squeezed the grapes into it, then I gave the cup to the king.”
text: na Farao no kɔɔpow kitsa me nsamu, na metsetseew wenyinamba no, na mikyii guu Farao no kɔɔpow mu, na medze kɔɔpow no hyɛɛ Farao ne nsa.
translated: and Pharaoh's cup was in my hand, and I took the grapes and pressed them into Pharaoh's cup, and I placed the cup in Pharaoh's hand.

ref: But when these good things happen, please don't forget to tell the king about me, so I can get out of this place.
text: Na sɛ inya beebi si a, ɔno kaa me, na yɛ me ayamuyie, meserɛ wo, na bɔ me dzin kyerɛ Farao, na beyi me fi dan yi mu:
translated: But if you remember me when things go well with you, please show me kindness and mention me to Pharaoh, and get me out of this prison:

ref: Joseph said: This is the meaning of your dream. The three baskets are three days,
text: Na Josef buae na ɔsee dɛ, Ne nkyerɛase nye yi: nkɛntsɛn ebiasa no yɛ ndaansa:
translated: And Joseph answered him saying, Its interpretation is this: the three branches are three days:
